medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient sent from PCP office with an acute drop in her Hct to 21 on labs drawn today. Baseline Hct is 30. Denies new symptoms; chronic mild abdominal pain, unchanged from baseline. Last bowel movement was ___ days prior to admission; chronic constipation managed with medications.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Chronic conditions: HTN, CKD, colon CA (s/p sigmoid colectomy), cholelithiasis, ventral hernia, multiple SBOs, lumbar spinal stenosis, OA, depression, gout.
medical_history.social_history: Denies substance use; bedbound for the past year due to falls.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: BP 121/47, HR 74, RR 16, Temp 98.2. Alert, oriented, no acute distress, speaking with a very loud voice. No acute distress noted.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: CNs grossly intact; strength decreased in left ankle (foot drop chronic, per patient). Cardiac: regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs. Abdomen: soft, mildly tender to palpation.
